headline: 167/384 (43%) final: 5/16 models reliable (>=20/24), 4 partial (29-71%), 6 excluded for non-pipeline causes (0-12.5%, never individually retried), 1 formally excluded after 3 dedicated retry rounds (llama-3.3-70b-instruct).
message: The 167/384 (43%) aggregate is not '1 model missing out of 16' -- it's the real performance spread across all 16. Only two models ever got individual root-cause diagnosis plus a dedicated retry round: nemotron-super-49b-v1.5 and llama-3.3-70b-instruct. The other 10 have sat untouched since the single D94b rate-limit retry pass. After the D96 correction and the weekly usage-limit reset, nemotron-super-49b-v1.5 was rerun with max_tokens=2048 and workers=4. Result: 23/24 (95.8%), Track B precision 1.0, clear strong/weak separation (5.0 vs 1.32) and self-correction differentiation (improving 4.5 vs weak 1.12) -- verified contamination-free. llama-3.3-70b-instruct was retried three separate times (workers=1, workers=4 across 7 distinct API keys, and workers=1 again at timeout_s=600) and stayed in the 0-20% range throughout. A controlled test hitting the model with 7 fully independent API keys simultaneously still saw 4/7 time out, ruling out any per-key or local explanation. A matching NVIDIA Developer Forums thread (different model, same error class) shows this is a known, unresolved upstream issue -- not specific to this pipeline. The user made the call to exclude this model and finalize the benchmark rather than keep retrying an infrastructure problem outside our control. The remaining 10 models were never given the same individual treatment: 4 are partial successes from the original rate-limit retry (minimax-m3 70.8%, qwen3.5-122b 58.3%, deepseek-v4-pro 41.7%, nemotron-3-super-120b 29.2%) and 6 sit at 0-12.5% with causes already identified as unrelated to rate limiting (gpt-oss-120b/gpt-oss-20b tool-choice non-compliance, mistral-nemotron server 500, mistral-large-3 hourly block, kimi-k2.6 account-permission 404, glm-5.2 undiagnosed) but were never retried after that diagnosis.
notes: nemotron-super-49b-v1.5: root cause (reasoning model, max_tokens starvation) confirmed via raw HTTP diagnostic in D94, fix confirmed via live rerun in D97.

llama-3.3-70b-instruct: root cause (NVIDIA worker-queue overload, HTTP 503 "153/16") confirmed and externally corroborated (NVIDIA Developer Forums, same error class on a different model, unresolved). Excluded from the final ranking -- see README.md D99.
Correction from the prior quota-contamination incident (D96) held: nemotron's successes were verified contamination-free before publishing.

See README.md D97-D99 for the full writeup, including a module-reload bug found along the way (two independent copies of the same script module via importlib caused CLIENT=None) and the new timeout_config.py + PreToolUse hook (D98) that centralizes and enforces the 600s timeout project-wide.

meta/llama-3.3-70b-instruct.top_error_summary: Persistent NVIDIA-side worker-queue overload across 3 retry rounds and a 7-key concurrent isolation test. Externally corroborated as a known, unresolved upstream issue. Formally excluded from the final 15/16 benchmark.

nvidia/llama-3.3-nemotron-super-49b-v1.5.top_error_summary: 1x remaining failure after two rounds of retry (non-timeout, cause not further diagnosed)
